name: High Point
description_short: Member-owned federal credit union serving personal and business members with checking, savings, loans, and digital banking since 1972.
description_long: High Point Federal Credit Union has operated as a member-owned, not-for-profit financial cooperative since 1972, positioning itself as a community-focused alternative to traditional banks. The institution serves both personal and business members with a comprehensive suite of financial products including deposit accounts, consumer lending, and mortgage services. High Point distinguishes itself through youth-focused programs including youth savings accounts, student loans, and a scholarship program, alongside business services such as business lending and merchant services. The credit union emphasizes digital accessibility with a modern online and mobile banking platform available 24/7, allowing members to manage accounts, make transfers, pay bills, and deposit checks remotely. High Point maintains physical locations (including a Bradford branch grand opening mentioned in 2026) and offers personalized financial tools and resources. The institution's marketing emphasizes life-stage financial partnerships and community involvement through volunteer opportunities and charitable donations. As a member-owned cooperative, High Point is regulated by the NCUA (National Credit Union Administration) rather than the FDIC, meaning member deposits are insured through Share Insurance rather than traditional deposit insurance. The credit union's routing number is 222381824, and membership appears to include both individual consumers and small business owners based on their service offerings and testimonials from business leaders.
services: Savings accounts and Money Market accounts
Youth savings accounts and student loans
Home equity loans and lines of credit
Digital banking with mobile app and online platform
pros: 24/7 digital banking access with integrated mobile app and online platform for convenient account management
Mobile check deposit feature with $275 immediate availability and two-business-day clearing for remaining balance
Comprehensive youth programs including youth savings accounts, student loans, and scholarship opportunities
Multiple checking account tiers (myBasic, myE-Checking, myRewards, myDividend) to serve different member needs
No-fee bill pay service with flexible scheduling (one-time or recurring) to reduce check writing and stamp costs
Business accounts and merchant services available for small business members alongside personal accounts
Community-focused mission with charitable donations and volunteer opportunities for member engagement
cons: Limited geographic presence with physical branches appearing concentrated in specific regions (Olean, Bradford area)
No mention of specific APRs, dividend rates, or competitive rate information on public-facing website content
Membership requirement and eligibility criteria not clearly detailed in available website content
Check deposit holds apply (balance available in two business days minimum), limiting immediate fund access for larger deposits
Limited transparency about fees for specific services like stop payments, debit card replacement, or account maintenance
best_for: Families seeking youth financial education programs and student loan options for their children
Small business owners needing integrated personal and business banking relationships with merchant services
Community-minded consumers prioritizing member-owned, not-for-profit financial institutions over traditional banks
Members in the Olean, New York region seeking local branch access and community-focused financial partnerships
diagnosis: High Point Federal Credit Union is best for community-focused consumers and small business owners in their service area seeking member-owned, not-for-profit banking with strong digital capabilities and youth programs. The main caveat is that membership eligibility and geographic availability may be limited compared to national banks, and specific rate and fee information requires direct member contact rather than public disclosure on their website.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: High Point — Credit Union in Olean, NY | CreditDoc
meta_description: High Point — NCUA-insured credit union in Olean, NY. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
